game_name: King's Quest VI: Heir Today, Gone Tomorrow

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Game start - Isle of the Crown
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Immediately taught through tutorial area after shipwreck
Early puzzles require simple item usage
Vizier Abdul Alhazred's scheme explained through dialogue

element_introduction_order.first_appears: After initial Isle of the Crown exploration
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Required to progress beyond starting island; enables open-world exploration

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Cliffs of Logic on Isle of the Sacred Mountain
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Requires consulting included 'Guidebook to the Land of the Green Isles' booklet; copy-protection mechanic
Players discover multiple valid approaches to same problems
Short path vs. long path choice; nearly 50% of content optional

element_introduction_order.element: Thematic island design (Arabian Nights, Alice in Wonderland, Classical mythology, Beauty and the Beast)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Each island introduces its own aesthetic puzzles and mechanics
Druid tribe areas and Death confrontation available to dedicated explorers

element_introduction_order.element: Multiple endings based on player choices
element_introduction_order.first_appears: End game - varies by path taken
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player informed of alternate choices upon completion; incentivizes replaying

typical_relief_triggers: Successful item usage solving a puzzle
Safe harbor areas between dangerous exploration zones
typical_tension_triggers: Encountering logic puzzles without clear solution path
Navigating magical barriers on Isle of the Beast
Choice points between short and long game paths
Optional content that player might miss
Multi-step puzzle chains requiring item combination
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Exploration-driven with puzzle-gated progression; player-controlled pacing through open-world island traversal; tension builds through accumulating unsolved puzzles that require synthesis of collected information and items

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Hidden areas accessible through exploration; druids and Death confrontation locations unmarked on magic map
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Magical barriers and environmental clues suggest hidden passages; thematic island design provides contextual hints (Beauty and Beast island heavily forested with magical obstacles)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Safe introduction on Isle of the Crown; basic point-and-click mechanics tutorialized; simple item collection and NPC interaction
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased puzzle complexity; multi-island navigation required; introduction of logic puzzles; external documentation consultation becomes necessary
difficulty_curve.late_game: Synthesis of collected knowledge and items; multi-step puzzle chains; optional challenging content (Death confrontation); multiple path culmination
notable_spikes: Cliffs of Logic requiring Guidebook consultation - gates access to Isle of the Sacred Mountain
Isle of the Beast magical barriers - navigation complexity spike
Labyrinth puzzle on Sacred Mountain - poem encoding from Guidebook required
Optional Death confrontation - difficulty spike for completionists

notable_level_patterns.description: Each island presents distinct aesthetic and mechanical themes inspired by literary sources (Arabian Nights, Alice in Wonderland, Classical mythology, Beauty and Beast), allowing players to mentally compartmentalize puzzle-solving approaches
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Isle of the Crown (Arabian theme), Isle of Wonder (Wonderland theme), Isle of the Sacred Mountain (Classical theme), Isle of the Beast (Beauty and Beast theme)

notable_level_patterns.description: Required external documentation (Guidebook) serves dual purpose as world-building flavor text and copy-protection mechanism; logic puzzles at Cliffs of Logic explicitly gate progression behind this requirement
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Cliffs of Logic gatekeeping access to Isle of the Sacred Mountain

notable_level_patterns.description: Nearly 50% of quests are optional with multiple solutions per puzzle; open-world design allows player-determined exploration order; explicit mid-game choice between short and long play paths
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Multiple island access routes; hidden druid and Death areas; branching endgame paths

notable_level_patterns.description: Inter-island travel exclusively via magic map after acquisition; replaces traditional walking navigation for island-to-island movement; spatial separation between islands reinforces thematic compartmentalization
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All transitions between Isle of Crown, Wonder, Sacred Mountain, and Beast islands
